dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 2 responding for breathing problems at 123 [STREET_ADDRESS] in [CITY].
Hi ma'am, I'm Jen, an EMT with the rescue squad. How have you been feeling lately?
I've had trouble breathing all night, and it just got worse when I went to bed.
Can you tell me your name and where we are right now please?
I see you're looking at me clearly.
Great, you're alert and oriented.
Can you squeeze my fingers for me please?
You're able to name yourself, know where you are, what the time is, and what has happened. So you are oriented to person, place, time, and situation.
I don't see any signs of airway obstruction. Is it easier to breathe in a certain position?
Respiration rate is 20 per minute. Breath sounds are diminished, no wheezes noted.
Peripheral pulses are present and strong.
Glasgow Coma Scale is 15, so you are alert and responsive.
You said it started around 10:30 PM. Can you tell me what happened after that?
Based on your record, I see you have COPD and diabetes, as well as anxiety, and bipolar disorder. Is there anything else I should know?
You're also noted with hyperlipidemia and a history of chronic pancreatitis. Has that been relevant to this episode?
When was the last time you had something to eat or drink?
You mentioned seasonal allergies. Have those been a problem tonight?
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is the pain in your right knee?
Your pulse is steady at 96 beats per minute.
Respirations are still 20 per minute.
Blood pressure is 144 over 90.
The glucose reading shows zero-did you eat or drink anything recently?
Oxygen saturation is 100% on 3 liters nasal cannula.
EKG findings are sinus rhythm with occasional premature atrial contractions.
I've started capnography. EtCO2 is reading at 65.
Placing on ECG 4 lead monitoring now.
Administering 0.5 mg of Atrovent via nebulizer. Is this the usual amount for you?
Albuterol 5 mg is going in now. Let's monitor your response.
We're proceeding with the respiratory distress protocol for possible COPD or asthma exacerbation.
EtCO2 is between 55 and 60 now-looks like some improvement.
You're still alert and oriented. How is your breathing now?
Your knee pain was reported at 10/10 earlier. Has there been any improvement?
You mentioned a moldy smell in your home. When did that occur?
Blood pressure has increased to 182 over 73 at this point.
A second EKG still shows sinus rhythm with PACs.
You haven't experienced any chest pain or abdominal pain with this episode.
I also note no signs of jugular vein distension.
We will continue with the COPD/asthma protocol and monitor for transport.
You mentioned not having your ID or insurance. Are you willing to sign for yourself?
Oxygen at 3 liters and nebulizer will continue en route.
We are heading to [COMPANY_NAME]-everything looks stable, and your breathing has improved.
Stretchers are secured and we're prepared for transport.
The airway protocol and oxygen are in place. Let's keep your head up and track EKG through the whole ride.
If your breathing worsens or changes, we'll adjust immediately. Any last questions or comments for us?
We've advised [CITY] of your condition and plans. All systems go to move.
We are on our way now.
